engine: Google Gemini 3 Pro (Preview) via Gemini CLI / AI Ultra
notes: Run of docs/PROMPT.md via the Gemini CLI authenticated with the user's Google AI Ultra account (OAuth, no API key / no prepay). Model gemini-3-pro-preview, executed per group (12 x 6). All 72 fixtures match the official draw.
prompt:
You are a quantitative football analyst. Predict ALL 72 group-stage matches of the
2026 FIFA World Cup (hosted by the USA, Canada and Mexico; 48 teams; 12 groups A–L;
played between June 11 and June 27, 2026). Use the OFFICIAL FIFA draw and schedule;
if you have web access, confirm the real groups, fixtures and dates.

For each match, weigh: squad strength and individual quality; FIFA ranking and recent
form (qualifiers + friendlies over the last 12 months); the form of key players in the
2025/26 club season; injuries/suspensions/final-squad cuts; tactical setup and the
manager's tournament record; head-to-head history; venue context (travel, time zones,
ALTITUDE in Mexico City, summer HEAT and HUMIDITY, pitch); home advantage for the
hosts; and market signals (betting odds).

For each match, derive the probability of a home win / draw / away win (summing to
100%) and the most likely scoreline. Be calibrated: prefer honest accuracy over false
confidence.

RESPOND WITH VALID JSON ONLY, no text before or after, in exactly this shape:

{
  "model": "claude",
  "predictions": [
    {
      "group": "A",
      "teamA": "<home team>",
      "teamB": "<away team>",
      "scoreA": <int>,
      "scoreB": <int>,
      "probWinA": <0-100>,
      "probDraw": <0-100>,
      "probWinB": <0-100>,
      "confidence": "high|medium|low",
      "reasoning": "<one sentence with the main signal>"
    }
  ]
}

Include all 72 group-stage matches. Do not invent teams, matches or statistics.

predictions.reasoning: Mexico's massive host advantage and higher technical ceiling will comfortably overcome a defensively organized but less potent South African side.
A clash of styles where South Korea's pace in transition is perfectly counterbalanced by the Czech Republic's physicality and aerial dominance on set-pieces.
South Korea's high energy will test the hosts, but Mexico's partisan crowd support and historical reliability in World Cup group stages provide the winning edge.
The Czechs' structured midfield and physical forwards will eventually break down South Africa's deep low block in a methodical, low-scoring affair.
High altitude and summer heat will heavily penalize the heavier European side, allowing Mexico to dictate the tempo and secure a narrow tactical victory.
South Korea's superior attacking quality, clinical finishing, and transitional speed will overwhelm a South African team forced to open up and chase points.
Canada's co-host advantage and pace in attack give them the edge over an aging Bosnian squad.
Canada's superior tactical discipline and athleticism will overpower Qatar.
Switzerland's midfield control will face a tough test against Canada's home crowd and counter-attacks, leading to a tight draw.
Bosnia's physical edge and European experience should be enough to secure a narrow victory.
Switzerland's structural solidity and big-tournament pedigree will easily dismantle Bosnia's defense.
A significant mismatch in quality across all areas of the pitch favors a comfortable Swiss win.
